description: Prompt where the bulk of the input is reference material — a doc, a transcript, a code blob, a long quote — and the actual task is a comparatively short directive about that material.

checks.description: Reference material is fenced (XML-style tag or triple-backtick / triple-quote) so the model can tell prompt from data.
Task verb appears within the first 30% of the prompt so the model knows what to look for as it reads the context.

checks.pattern: \b(only use|based on|from|cite|quote|reference|do not invent|stick to|grounded in|according to)\b
checks.description: Explicit instruction to ground the answer in the supplied context (anti-hallucination guardrail).
Length is constrained so the model doesn't dump the whole context back at the user.

checks.pattern: \b(quote the entire|repeat the full|reproduce the whole|copy the whole)\b
checks.description: Doesn't ask the model to repeat the context wholesale.
Prompt is long enough to plausibly carry real context (≥200 chars). Below that this is probably a task_only mislabel.
